# Iris

> A portable runtime for durable AI agents. You declare an agent as a config file plus
> a folder (instructions, tools, skills); `iris build` compiles it into an open,
> content-addressed image you run anywhere. At runtime a session is an event-sourced
> journal — so it is durable (a crash loses nothing) and portable (pause on one host,
> resume on another, byte-for-byte identical), against any LLM, over any channel.

## Getting started
- [Introduction](docs/introduction.md): what Iris is, and the one idea — a session is a journal.
- [Your first agent](docs/first-agent.md): init → build → chat with no API key, then a real model; resume across a restart.
- [Tools](docs/tools.md): tools as versioned contracts referenced by address; the tool boundary; secrets & environment.
- [Channels](docs/channels.md): durable, resumable sessions over HTTP (SSE/WebSocket), the web chat UI, the client SDK.
- [Deploy](docs/deploy.md): resume the same session on a different host — one command to a Cloudflare Durable Object.

## Concepts
- [The harness](docs/harness.md): the model↔tool loop as swappable tactics at five seams (assembleContext, shouldCompact, decideNext, gateAction, onToolError); every decision is journaled, so tactics can be third-party/nondeterministic and replay still can't diverge. Default bundle + the coding bundle; configure via harness.bundle / harness.tactics.
- [Models & providers](docs/providers.md): vendor-neutral, replay-safe model adapters; how to add one.
- [Governance & approvals](docs/governance.md): the approval gate, the policy layer, the journaled approval trail.
- [Audit & reproducible evals](docs/audit-and-evals.md): iris audit; reproducible evals; subagents & schedules.
- [Verifiable portable journals](docs/verifiable-journal.md): content-addressed *.irisjournal export/verify/import.

## Reference
- [Channel-port spec](docs/reference/channel-port-spec.md): the contract every channel passes.
- [Bridge pattern](docs/reference/bridge-pattern.md): Discord/Telegram/Teams as external bridges.
- [Verifiable-journal format](docs/reference/verifiable-journal-spec.md): the export format, reproducible from the file alone.
- [Journal threat model](docs/reference/threat-model.md): what journal verification detects, and what it does not claim.
- [Sandbox-egress threat model](docs/reference/security-sandbox-threat-model.md): the egress firewall + credential broker.

## Discovery
- [agents.md](agents.md): how an AI agent or tool integrates with Iris — Agentfile schema, MCP server, REST API.
- [README](README.md): the project manifesto (why Iris exists).
